compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: CLAIMED by RebarBotNava (2026-07-15). Plan-review journey: v1→v4 (kappa protocol → §5 report locatability + case-ticket pinning → dropped-findings source correction → exact positional coverage.counts bucketing + event-scan predicates for the four counts). Plan PASSED at v4; 19 advisories to honor in implementation (positional DROPPED slice must skip the indeterminate segment; fixed random seed; LLM-rater retry/backoff; canonical atomic-write helper). Attestation/HEAD note: unscoped plan-review attestations require signed_head==worktree HEAD while review stamps signed_head=origin/main, so claimed from HEAD==origin/main (b0ac2544) with the recovered SOTA report kept as an on-disk untracked file (now committing post-claim). Epic 6982 also claimed (in_progress). Next: implement mine_outcome_corpus.py (event-scan) → outcome_corpus.jsonl + §5 re-verify; then build_adjudication_corpus.py + rubrics + kappa.py; then README + land via Gerrit.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: IMPLEMENTATION FINDING (data recovery, 2026-07-15): the on-disk active+.retired scan is INSUFFICIENT for compacted tickets. Verified on dc58-af7b-034c-4a4e (an older ticket): its dir holds only {2 COMMENT, 1 EDIT, 1 REVIEW_RESULT, 1 SNAPSHOT} — CREATE + all STATUS events were folded into the SNAPSHOT with NO .retired STATUS/CREATE files on disk. So reopen_count, the post-claim-edit window, and ticket_type CANNOT be recovered from on-disk events for compacted tickets. This confirms report §5's method (git-object walk across all tickets-branch refs — 15,802 event files) is the REQUIRED primary recovery path, not a fallback (the subagent's '.retired gives full history' held in aggregate but not for old folded tickets). Recovery design: (a) SNAPSHOT.data.compiled_state (23-key reduced state) gives ticket_type/level/last_reopened_at; SNAPSHOT.data.source_event_uuids lists folded event uuids; (b) 'git -C .tickets-tracker rev-list --objects --all' + 'git cat-file' recovers the historical STATUS/EDIT/CREATE blobs (dc58: 3 STATUS/CREATE blobs recoverable), dedup by uuid, group by ticket_id from the blob path. STATUS shape confirmed {status,current_status}; EDIT changes live in data.fields (NOT data.description). WIP: docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md (recovered, uncommitted on disk) + harnesses/mine_outcome_corpus.py (v1, on-disk-scan — being reworked to git-object walk). Both tickets (E1 + epic 6982) are claimed/in_progress under RebarBotNava.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
SCOPE EXPANSION (operator-approved, this session 2026-07-15) — validity basis moves from an LLM-only kappa gate to HUMAN-ADJUDICATED ground truth.

WHY: the A/B Cohen's kappa fell 0.74 -> 0.60 -> 0.50 as the usable-pair count grew. A kappa that decays with n is the small-sample-luck signature; true LLM inter-rater agreement here is genuinely moderate (~0.5), i.e. adjudicating gate findings as TP/FP is substantively subjective for a real fraction of findings. That is itself a calibration-relevant finding (a noise band exists), not a bug to tune away. Continuing to edit rubrics until kappa clears 0.7 would be training-on-the-test-set (a tuned kappa is a worthless trust signal), AND the original plan already sanctioned a failed gate as a documentable outcome — so kappa-chasing was both scope creep and a threat to corpus integrity. Operator directed: invest to reach a genuinely-usable corpus via human adjudication of the disputed labels over a constrained sample.

NEW VALIDITY BASIS (replaces the kappa>=0.7 pass/fail gate; AC edit pending before close):
1. Report A/B kappa HONESTLY as the LLM-reproducibility signal, stratified by source (surfaced vs dropped) — disclosure, not a ship/discard gate. (Dropped-lens polarity is the dominant confound: "spurious+dropped = good drop = TP" inverts the surfaced lens; one rubric clarification was applied to remove that genuine ambiguity, disclosed, and will NOT be iterated further.)
2. HUMAN ADJUDICATION of the contested findings is the authoritative ground truth: A/B disagreements are clustered into distinct dispute archetypes (near-duplicate findings within the same source+criterion collapse to one representative; the human verdict propagates to all members, every member's text shown so propagation is auditable) so the operator adjudicates each DISTINCT dispute once, never the same boilerplate finding repeatedly. Operator adjudicates -> folded back as the final gold label.

HARNESS ROBUSTNESS FIX (found while investigating): adjudicate.py did a single terminal atomic write — all-or-nothing over a ~500-call, ~25-min serialized pass, which also defeated its own resumable design (a mid-run death persisted zero labels; observed: 0 bytes on disk after hundreds of billable calls). Fixed with incremental atomic checkpointing every 5 labels (crash-safe + resumable-for-real); named the append-only-journal / keyed-store pattern as the answer for a genuinely large corpus. Smoke-tested green.

NEW/CHANGED ARTIFACTS: adjudicate.py (checkpointing); cluster_disputes.py (dispute archetype clustering + human worksheet); apply_adjudications.py (fold human verdicts -> final gold labels); README validity section rewritten; kappa.py stays (now reporting, stratified) not a hard gate.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
CORPUS-QUALITY FINDING (2026-07-15) — text-less findings excluded; adjudication corpus rebuilt.

While preparing the human-adjudication worksheet, found that 61/400 sampled findings (15%) had an EMPTY `finding` statement — only metadata (location/severity/criteria) + a `verification` block, no prose. Root cause: finding PROSE (`finding`/`suggested_fix`) is persisted by `_slim` only since story 4e19/e344 (sidecar.py:441-446); review results written before that carry a leaner schema with no statement. Not recoverable (the text was never persisted, so a git-object walk can't recover it either).

WHY IT MATTERS: a finding with no statement is NOT adjudicatable by ANY rater — an LLM or human judging TP/FP from location+criterion alone is guessing. Those 20/110 text-less rows in the double-labeled subset were almost certainly inflating disagreement (depressing kappa) with noise.

FIX: build_adjudication_corpus.collect() now EXCLUDES findings with empty `finding` (documented inclusion rule + per-source exclusion count returned). Verified text-bearing population is ample: 15,821 surfaced + 10,850 dropped (excluded text-less: 2,592 surfaced + 708 dropped storewide), so the 300+100 targets and ≥280/≥90 floor still hold comfortably. Rebuilt the corpus as a clean seed-1729 stratified sample of the text-bearing population (300 surfaced + 100 dropped, 0 text-less, all rows carry a statement). Cost: the filtered population reshuffled the deterministic sample (only 4/400 rows overlapped the prior draw), so the ~500 prior LLM labels are largely sunk and a full re-label is running — accepted as the price of a correct, adjudicatable corpus.

ALSO FIXED en route: (1) adjudicate.py max_tokens 400->2000 — opus-4-8/sonnet-5 think by default and a 400-cap was fully consumed by thinking (stop_reason=max_tokens, thinking_tokens=399) before any text emitted, silently failing ~15% of rows (this, not rate-limiting, was the persistent-failure cause); (2) cluster_disputes classify bug — rows outside the double-labeled subset (rater_b blank) were mis-flagged as 'soft disputes'; a dispute now requires BOTH raters to have judged the row.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
OPERATOR ATTESTATION — human adjudication of the 30 contested plan-review findings (2026-07-16).

WHO: the operator (a human), adjudicating live via AskUserQuestion across 6 rounds this session.
WHAT: all 30 A/B disputes in runs/disputes.jsonl ruled — TALLY 16 TP / 14 FP. Each disputed finding was shown with both raters' calls and a FULL-PLAN context summary (correcting the adjudication harness's 6000-char PLAN_CAP that had starved the LLM raters on long plans and produced several of the 'ambiguous' rows).

METHOD/PROVENANCE (auditable in runs/disputes.jsonl `human_label` + `human_label_basis`):
- 23 disputes ruled individually by the operator.
- 3 near-duplicate archetypes semantically GROUPED (one ruling -> both members, all member text shown): H013+H015 (parent advisory-disposition gate vs children -> FP), H003+H004 (no proving command in ACs, dropped -> TP good-drop), H011+H012 (false code-fact rationale -> TP real).
- 4 clear nit/spurious/content-free disputes INFERRED from the operator's own prior authoritative rulings and EXPLICITLY CONFIRMED by the operator (Confirm-4 round, no vetoes): H005, H009 (TP good-drop), H016 (FP noise), S003 (TP good-drop).

Two findings were fact-checked against the code before ruling: S001 (event_append.py has its OWN write-side EVENT_TYPES frozenset, distinct from the reducer's -> the plan's file_impact omission is a real escaped defect -> FP) and H020 (Rater A's 'cited locations absent' rationale was factually wrong; the plan does provision the reviewbot-tickets-pat -- but the operator still ruled FP as a non-improving nit).

FOLD: harnesses/apply_adjudications.py folded the verdicts to final_label/label_source on every corpus row -- 0 pending-human, 379 gold definite labels (245 TP / 134 FP), 20 ambiguous, 1 unlabeled (a failed Rater-A call). This human adjudication (NOT the LLM kappa) is the authoritative validity basis for the corpus, per the operator-approved scope change. Kappa is reported honestly for transparency (overall kappa 0.47 / Gwet's AC1 0.58 / raw agreement 0.77; stratified in runs/README.md).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
CORPUS INVALIDATION + CORRECTED DESIGN (operator-flagged, 2026-07-16). The finding-adjudication corpus LABELS are INVALID and Gerrit change 735 is ABANDONED.

ROOT CAUSE (two orthogonal confounds, both in the adjudication tooling, NEITHER in production):
1. TRUNCATION: adjudicate.py capped plan text at PLAN_CAP=6000 chars; production (orchestrator.assemble_context) reviews the WHOLE plan + children, never truncated. 56% of the 400 findings sit on plans >6000 chars -> the rater saw less than production.
2. NO GROUNDING: adjudicate.py ran a single messages.create with NO tools; production runs 16 criteria (A1,E4,G1G2,G3,G4,G6,G7,T1,T3,T5c,T8,T10,T11,T13,T14,removal-rationale) as AGENTIC finders that grep the codebase (pai_tools.filesystem_tools+grounding_tools+rebar_tools). 55% of findings are on such criteria; the rater could not do what production does.
COMBINED: 316/400 (79%) findings contaminated; only 84 (21%) methodologically clean. kappa subset 82% contaminated.

HUMAN ADJUDICATION IS ALSO INVALIDATED (operator's point): the 30 disputes were SELECTED by the faulty A/B process (both truncated+tool-less), the human was ANCHORED on faulty rater rationales, and 17/30 disputes are on agentic criteria where neither rater nor the human systematically grounded in code. So the label layer (LLM + human), disputes.jsonl, dispute_worksheet.md, the kappa figures, and the README validity section are all invalid.

SURVIVES: outcome_corpus.jsonl (mined from events, never LLM-labeled), the finding SAMPLE (production outputs; the stratified 300+100 draw is reusable), the harness/rubrics/SOTA report as machinery.

CORRECTED APPROACH (reuse production code so the adjudicator is a faithful/stronger analog): (a) whole context via orchestrator.assemble_context (no cap, includes children); (b) code-claim classification is the DETERMINISTIC plan_review_grounding op (keyed on the criterion's registered grounding), not an LLM guess; (c) GROUNDED judging via get_runner(cfg) in execution_mode=agentic bound to the finding's REVIEW-TIME code root, reusing pai_tools -> the adjudicator greps real code; (d) validity/impact via the production decide layer (pass3_over_findings/impact_plan/decide.validity). HUMAN audit stays the authoritative ground truth, shown the GROUNDED evidence (not raw rationales); reliability becomes grounded-verifier-vs-human agreement (which localizes the Pass-2 sub-questions/axes that miscalibrate). No new gate, no truncation, no tool-less pass, no multi-unreliable-rater panel. E1 stays in_progress; the corrected corpus replaces the abandoned one in a fresh change.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: HAND-ADJUDICATION ERROR FOUND (S001) — surfaced by the corrected grounded adjudicator prototype. During adjudication I ruled S001 (ticket e165, finding 'event_append.py EVENT_TYPES not in file_impact') FP = escaped defect, claiming I had 'verified' it against code. I verified the CODE fact (event_append.py line 54 does have its own EVENT_TYPES frozenset) but did NOT read the whole plan — exactly the truncated rater's failure. e165's plan SECTION 5 'Write-path allowlist' (at char ~8166, PAST the 6000-char PLAN_CAP) already states: 'src/rebar/_store/event_append.py: KEY_ADD/KEY_REVOKE MUST be added to the hardcoded EVENT_TYPES frozenset ... or _validate_event raises a StoreError ... Separate from the reducer known-type set in _version.py — BOTH must be updated.' So the plan already covers the substance; the finding is a restatement absent only from the file_impact ARRAY, not an escaped defect. The grounded verifier (whole plan + real code grep) scored it validity 0.375 -> correctly dropped. Concrete proof the human layer was contaminated by the same 'didn't see the whole plan' failure — reinforces the full rebuild.

compiled_state.description:
## DESCOPE (2026-07-16) — finding-adjudication corpus removed; outcome corpus retained
The finding-adjudication corpus (steps 3-5 below) was INVALIDATED: it was labeled by a tool-less, plan-truncated LLM process that does not match production (whole-plan, code-grounded, agentic), so 79% of the TP/FP labels are contaminated and the human adjudication inherited the flaw (see session log + ticket comments; Gerrit 735 abandoned). A production-faithful rebuild was prototyped but rejected as duplicative of the gate's own sidecar evidence + over the human-time budget. E1 is therefore rescoped to its VALID, independently-useful deliverables: the SOTA report, the outcome corpus + mining harness (steps 0-2), and the reusable finding SAMPLE (the sampler, unlabeled). The independent human-adjudicated TP/FP gold set is parked as idea e59d-c078-724d-46e5 (brutal-idiocratic-rodent, relates_to this epic) for future enrichment. Dependents are unaffected: E2/E3/R1/R2/R7 consume the OUTCOME corpus (valid); E6 consumes the finding SAMPLE (valid) to measure self-consistency (it re-judges findings; it never needed the TP/FP labels).

---

## Why
Every gate row in epic pastoral-aquatic-viper adjudicates against ground truth that does not yet exist in frozen form. The report's §5 claims (16/505 post-claim edits, 3/8 review misses in one defect class) must be re-verified, not trusted, and the finding-level TP/FP labels needed by E2/E3/E6 and R1/R7 need a committed, versioned corpus. This story only *freezes* those corpora and the validity gate; it changes no gate/source code (nothing under `src/`).

## What
Freeze two ground-truth corpora under `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/` with a versioned README, land the SOTA report as their committed provenance source, and commit the mining/adjudication/kappa scripts, in these steps:

0. **Land the §5 provenance report.** This change commits `docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md`, recovered verbatim from commit `143d5074e` (authored by task d39b / englacial-communal-conure, which **closed without landing it to main**). The report is the published baseline the `--verify-s5` re-verification table compares against, so it must be locatable in-repo. (Done as the first commit of this change.)

1. **Mine the outcome corpus** — one row per reviewed ticket (~527 reviewed tickets), written atomically (`.tmp` + `os.replace`) to `runs/outcome_corpus.jsonl`.
   - **Data source (verified — no git-object walk needed):** the mining script scans the committed on-disk event files under `.tickets-tracker/<ticket_id>/`. Compaction renames folded events to `<name>.json.retired` and **those `.retired` files are committed on the tickets branch**, so scanning both `*-<TYPE>.json` and `*-<TYPE>.json.retired` yields the full event history reproducibly, without any `~/.claude/jobs/*` artifact and without raw git-object walking (`git rev-list --objects --all` + `git cat-file` remains an equivalent authoritative fallback, noted in the README). Plan-review and completion sidecars are read through their clean public reader APIs (`rebar.llm.plan_review.sidecar.all_review_results`, `rebar.llm.completion_sidecar`), which are reducer-ignored and compaction-preserved.
   - **Pre-flight completeness probe (mandatory):** before writing, the script counts distinct reviewed tickets discovered on disk; if that count falls below a floor of **500** (expected ~527) it halts with an actionable error (`tickets history incomplete`) and writes nothing.
   - **Outcome-corpus schema** (documented in the README): `{ticket_id, ticket_type, level, post_claim_edit_class, reopen_count (int), force_close (bool), completion_verifier_fail_count (int, retention-capped ≤10), review_round_count (int, retention-capped ≤50), had_persisted_review (bool)}`. `had_persisted_review` is load-bearing: Step 3 samples findings only from tickets where it is true.
   - **Exact per-count derivation (grounded in the event model — there is NO REOPEN/FORCE_CLOSE event type; all transitions are `STATUS` events):**
     - `reopen_count` = number of `STATUS` events with `data.current_status=="closed"` and `data.status=="open"` (`rebar reopen` == `transition closed open`).
     - `force_close` = true iff any `COMMENT` event has `data.body` starting with `"FORCE_CLOSE:"` (force-close writes no STATUS flag; it leaves a `FORCE_CLOSE:` comment + a missing completion signature).
     - `completion_verifier_fail_count` = number of `COMPLETION_VERDICT` events with `data.schema=="completion_verifier_fail_v1"` (retention-capped at 10/ticket — noted as a bound in the README).
     - `review_round_count` = `len(sidecar.all_review_results(ticket_id))` (retention-capped at 50/ticket).
     - `had_persisted_review` = `review_round_count > 0`.
   - **`post_claim_edit_class` closed vocabulary** (the §5.2 taxonomy; exactly one per row): `none`, `cosmetic`, `premise-invalidated`, `scope-reduction`, `approach-change`, `plan-authored-post-claim`, `ac-strengthened`, `operator-attested-retag`. An edit is a DESCRIPTION-mutating event (`EDIT`/`CREATE`-superseding description change) on the ticket.
   - **Post-claim window (reopen-safe):** an edit is *post-claim* iff its event timestamp falls in `[first STATUS→in_progress, first STATUS→closed]`. For reopened tickets, edits in any post-reopen window are **not** classified here (the §5 signal is the first claim→close cycle); `reopen_count` is recorded separately.
   - **Overlapping-class precedence (first match wins):** `plan-authored-post-claim` (no `## Acceptance Criteria` block at first-claim, one appeared post-claim) → `premise-invalidated` → `approach-change` → `scope-reduction` → `ac-strengthened` → `operator-attested-retag` → `cosmetic` → `none`. Example: a ticket with a plan at claim that post-claim gained an `[operator-attested]` AC tag = `operator-attested-retag`; a ticket claimed with an empty description that gained its whole plan post-claim = `plan-authored-post-claim`.

2. **Re-verify the §5 headline claims** against the frozen outcome corpus (report §5.2). `mine_outcome_corpus.py --verify-s5` prints the derived figures; the README re-verification table and the ticket comment record each derived figure **beside** the §5 published figure marked `AGREES` or `CORRECTED-TO <n>` per figure (never a vacuous "agreement or corrected numbers"). Figures: post-claim-edit rate (§5 **16/505 = 3.2%**), substantive share (§5 **15/16**), persisted-review subset (§5 **8**), the **3 MISSED** asserted-capability cases (§5 **dc58-af7b, db7b-c8fd, 5886-d028**), the **4 CAUGHT-BUT-IGNORED** (**c8cc-68b8, f5df-0069, 115b-ceea, 8c4f-b81c**), the **1 UNKNOWABLE** (**3006-e198**). All eight case tickets are confirmed present in the store (`rebar show` resolves each). **Disambiguation:** §5's `db7b` is story `db7b-c8fd` ("REVIEW_RESULT reducer-ignored sidecar") — it shares its short id with the sidecar it discusses; the corpus and table pin full 4-segment ids.

3. **Build the finding-adjudication corpus** — committed script `harnesses/build_adjudication_corpus.py` samples plan-review findings from the persisted `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar payloads (via `all_review_results`), restricted to tickets with `had_persisted_review=true`. Target **~300 surfaced + ~100 dropped** findings, stratified by criterion id and by bucket, written to `runs/adjudication_corpus.jsonl`. Floor for the AC: **≥280 surfaced and ≥90 dropped**; if the population is smaller, all available are taken and actual counts recorded.
   - **Exact surfaced/dropped bucketing (verified — `decision` alone is INSUFFICIENT):** a finding's `decision` cannot classify it, because overflow-suppressed advisories carry `decision="advisory"` (identical to surfaced advisories) and novelty-dropped findings also keep `decision="advisory"`. Instead the sidecar payload stores `findings[]` in a fixed positional order whose segment lengths are exactly `coverage.counts = {blocking, advisory_surfaced, advisory_overflow, dropped, indeterminate}`: `findings = blocking ++ advisory_surfaced ++ overflow ++ indeterminate ++ dropped`. The script buckets by **position**: `SURFACED = findings[: blocking + advisory_surfaced]` (shown to the agent); `DROPPED = the overflow segment ++ the trailing dropped segment` (sidecar-only, suppressed). This is exact and independent of the `decision`/`drop_reason` ambiguity.
   - **Adjudication-corpus schema** (documented in the README; it deliberately **diverges** from `code_review_adjudication.jsonl`'s `{block-worthy, not-block-worthy, ambiguous}` vocabulary — this corpus uses TP/FP): `{finding_id, ticket_id, criterion, criteria[], source ∈ {surfaced, dropped}, decision, drop_reason (nullable), tp_fp ∈ {TP, FP, ambiguous}, rater_a, rater_b (nullable), rationale, impact, validity, priority}`.
   - **TP/FP definition (co-located in README):** for a `surfaced` finding, `TP` = the criterion genuinely applies (correct surface), `FP` = spurious; for a `dropped` finding, `TP` = correctly suppressed (no real defect), `FP` = a real defect the gate suppressed. `ambiguous` rows are excluded pairwise from kappa.

4. **Double-label and compute inter-rater reliability.** Two **independent LLM raters** adjudicate:
   - **Rater A (primary):** LLM adjudicator with rubric `harnesses/adjudicate_rubric_a.md`, labels the full sample `TP/FP/ambiguous` (populates `tp_fp`, `rater_a`).
   - **Rater B (independent):** a second LLM adjudicator with an **independently-authored** rubric `harnesses/adjudicate_rubric_b.md` and a **different model id** from Rater A, re-labels a **stratified-by-criterion ≥50-finding subset BLIND** to Rater A (populates `rater_b`). Rubric B is worded independently so kappa measures genuine agreement, not shared-prompt echo.
   - **Rubric contract (both files):** each states the TP/FP definitions verbatim, instructs criterion-aware judgment (the finding's `criteria` + ticket plan are provided as context), and fixes output as one JSON object per finding `{finding_id, tp_fp ∈ {TP,FP,ambiguous}, rationale}`.
   - **Kappa:** committed `harnesses/kappa.py` computes **Cohen's kappa** on the binary `{TP, FP}` labels of the double-labeled subset (ambiguous excluded pairwise), prints the value; README records it with both rater/model identities.

5. **Apply the validity gate.** kappa ≥ 0.7 → labels are usable ground truth. kappa < 0.7 → labels are declared **unusable**, `kappa.py` exits non-zero, README + ticket comment document the failed validity gate; no labels are shipped as ground truth for downstream stories.

## Scope
- `docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md` (landed from 143d5074e — §5 provenance baseline).
- `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/outcome_corpus.jsonl`, `runs/adjudication_corpus.jsonl` (new).
- `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md` (new/extended): both schemas, the `post_claim_edit_class` + `tp_fp` vocabularies, the reopen-window + precedence rules, the positional-bucketing rule, the retention-cap notes, the §5 re-verification table, the recorded kappa + rater/model identities, provenance, and the **exact regeneration commands**.
- `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/mine_outcome_corpus.py`, `build_adjudication_corpus.py`, `kappa.py`, `adjudicate_rubric_a.md`, `adjudicate_rubric_b.md` (new; committed **alongside** `corpus_sample.py`). They do **not** reuse corpus_sample.py's ephemeral `TMP` scratch paths — they write final artifacts under `runs/` and scan `.tickets-tracker/` on disk. `mine_outcome_corpus.py` exposes `--dry-run` (smoke) and `--verify-s5`.
- NO gate/source changes (nothing under `src/`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md` is committed (the §5 provenance baseline) and its §5 published figures are the ones the re-verification table cites.
- [ ] `runs/outcome_corpus.jsonl` is committed (one row per reviewed ticket carrying `post_claim_edit_class` + the four event-derived counts), `harnesses/mine_outcome_corpus.py` is committed with `--dry-run` exit 0, and `runs/README.md` documents the schema, retention caps, and the exact regeneration command. Proving command: `python .../mine_outcome_corpus.py --dry-run` exits 0 and `wc -l runs/outcome_corpus.jsonl` >= 500.
- [ ] `mine_outcome_corpus.py --verify-s5` re-derives the §5 figures and the README re-verification table records, per figure, the derived value beside the §5 published value marked `AGREES` or `CORRECTED-TO <n>`.
- [ ] The reusable finding SAMPLE machinery is committed: `harnesses/build_adjudication_corpus.py` (the positional-`coverage.counts` sampler, stratified) + the UNLABELED sample (findings only, no `tp_fp`), for downstream reuse (E6's self-consistency cohort). Proving command: a python check that the sample has >=280 surfaced + >=90 dropped text-bearing findings, each with `source` + `criterion`, and NO shipped `tp_fp`/kappa artifacts.
- [ ] The finding-level TP/FP LABELING is explicitly DESCOPED to idea `e59d-c078-724d-46e5`: the invalid tool-less/truncated labels, `disputes.jsonl`, `dispute_worksheet.md`, and the kappa/validity sections are NOT shipped, and `runs/README.md` records the invalidation rationale + the pointer to the idea.
compiled_state.title: E1: freeze plan-review ground-truth corpora (outcome + finding adjudication, kappa gate)
